pattern: Exhaustively combine four numbers two at a time with the four arithmetic operations, recursing on the reduced multiset until one value remains and checking for twenty-four within floating tolerance.
hints: There are only four numbers, so a brute-force search over every way to pick a pair and an operator is small enough.
After combining two chosen numbers into one result, you are left with a smaller collection to solve recursively the same way.
Order matters for subtraction and division, division by zero must be skipped, and the final comparison to twenty-four needs a tiny epsilon because of floating-point rounding.

edge_inputs.description: all nines cannot reach the target

stress.description: random four-card hands across the legal value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    return [[rng.randint(1, 9) for _ in range(4)]]
stress.note: size ignored; arity is fixed at four

stress.description: hands biased toward small numbers where fractional paths matter
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    return [[rng.randint(1, 4) for _ in range(4)]]
